findings.detail: Seven nodes (A1, A2, A3, B1, B2, B3, B4) in two direction-TB subgraphs under a flowchart LR root. All bold labels are 1–3 words. Every label traces directly to corpus §5.3–§6: 'Call Site' (§6 call site), 'Phase 1: Compute' (§5.4 Phase 1), 'Phase 2: Write' (§5.4 Phase 2), 'compute_average()' (§6 refactored function names), 'Average returned' (§5.5 return + pass result), 'write_average_to_file()' (§6 refactored function names). No downstream topics (decorators, classes, *args, list comprehensions) appear. Title 'One Function = One Job — the Testable Unit Principle' sits in the config title: field — correct per title-discipline rule. Each subgraph is a clean single-path linear flow (no branching). BEFORE→AFTER cross-subgraph edge labelled 'refactor into' makes the pedagogical contrast explicit. classDef tiers: callsite (blue #a5d8ff, neutral — appears in both panels as the call site anchor), multi (warm orange #ffd8a8 / stroke #f76707 — problem-side phases), single (purple #d0bfff — solution functions), ret (green #b2f2bb — return value connector). Subgraph backgrounds differentiated: BEFORE warm (#fff4e6 / amber stroke) for problem panel, AFTER cool (#f0fff4 / green stroke) for solution panel — matches before/after intent.

Side-by-side panel layout (LR root + direction-TB subgraphs) exactly matches the diagram intent: 'Before/after contrast: multi-job function vs the same logic split into two single-job functions'. Follows the canonical 12-1 / 12-2 / 2-4 golden-dataset pattern. BEFORE→AFTER edge with label 'refactor into' makes the transformation direction explicit and beginner-readable.

All 7 nodes map exclusively to §5.3 (Testable Unit), §5.4 (Multi-Job identification — Phase 1/Phase 2), §5.5 (Refactoring steps), and §6 (Implementation — compute_average, write_average_to_file, call site). 'Testable independently' appears in B4 subtitle, traced to §5.3 definition. No advanced Python (decorators, generators, classes, *args) present.
